sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Kenneth, a frequent water skier, reports a 5-day history of right knee pain after twisting his knee while landing a jump on his boat on Lake Martin.
He describes the pain as sore, with swelling and limited mobility, but denies any numbness or tingling in his foot.
He has taken Aleve twice a day and applied a cold pack to alleviate the pain, which has provided some relief.
The patient has never had a similar injury to this knee before.

Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, for which he is currently taking lisinopril 20mg daily, and has been managing his blood pressure well, with readings of 125/80 at home.
He also had his tonsils removed in the past.
Review of systems: The patient reports no other symptoms, but is excited for the upcoming spring season and outdoor activities.
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure in the office is 120/77, which is within a normal range.
Physical exam: On examination, the patient's neck, heart, and lung exams are unremarkable.
However, the musculoskeletal exam reveals ecchymosis, edema, and an effusion on the right knee, with pain to palpation and decreased range of motion.
The varus and valgus tests are negative, and the Lachman sign is also negative.
The patient's lower extremities are otherwise unremarkable.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain in the right knee, which is likely the result of a twisting injury sustained while water skiing.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain; Hypertension; Past: Tonsillectomy.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg once daily to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the right knee.
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any fractures.
I also referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury.
The patient is to rest the knee and avoid further activity until the results of the x-ray are available.
I continued the patient's current lisinopril regimen for hypertension and ordered a lipid panel to monitor the patient's lipid profile.
I also administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen again after the results of the x-ray are available, and the patient will follow up with physical therapy as scheduled.
